title: ManifestResponse
description: Complete command tree for an agent to discover all available commands, their flags, exit codes, and examples in a single call — without iterating --help per subcommand.

schema_version.description: Version of the ManifestResponse schema itself. Increment the major when field shape changes in a breaking way.

framework_version.description: Version of the CLI framework or tool binary.

etag.description: Stable content hash of the manifest. Changes only when command registrations change. Agents pass this back via --etag to receive a cache hit instead of recomputing the full manifest.

commands.description: Flat map from dot-separated command path to CommandEntry. 'deploy.rollback' is the rollback subcommand of deploy. Every registered command appears here, including built-ins.

description.description: One-sentence summary of what the command does.

aliases.description: Alternative names this command can be invoked with.

flags.description: Map from flag name (without leading --) to FlagEntry.

exit_codes.description: Map from exit code integer (as string key) to ExitCodeEntry. Sourced from REQ-C-001 declarations.

subcommands.description: Dot-separated paths of direct child commands. Resolve each via the top-level commands map.

type.description: Scalar type of the flag value.

required.description: True if the flag must be provided for the command to proceed.

description.description: What the flag controls, including valid range or format constraints.

default.description: Value used when the flag is omitted. Omit this property (do not set null) when there is no default.

enum_values.description: Exhaustive list of accepted values. Present only when type is 'enum'.

short.description: Single-character shorthand (e.g. 'n' for --dry-run). Omit if none exists.

description.description: One-line description of what this example demonstrates.

command.description: Full invocation string an agent can use verbatim.
